clauses_raw.clause_text_raw: The DW zone is the heart of Charlottetown's urban waterfront along the Hillsborough River Estuary. The waterfront's direct connection to the ocean means it is home to a variety of pleasure craft, cruise ships and ships large and small. The waterfront boardwalk continues to evolve to reserve the open space corridor for the public along the water's edge from the Culinary Institute to the Cruise Port. There is some concern over the impacts of sea level rise on the waterfront.
Rezoning to another zone is not permitted in the DW Zone.
The following uses are permitted in the DW Zone:
Lodging House
Commercial Daycare Centre
Community Care Facility
Community Institutional
Cultural Establishment
Educational Institution (private)
Educational Institution (public)
Fire Station
Funeral Establishment
Government Offices
Library
Medical, Health and Dental Office
Police Station
Recreation or Fitness Center (indoors)
Convenience Store
Eating and Drinking Establishment
Entertainment Establishment
Grocery Store
Hostel / Hotel
Office

These uses are permitted so long as the following lot requirements are met: the front yard setback may be greater than the max. for up to 30% of the streetline the flankage yard setback may be greater than the max. for up to 30% of the streetline
Rear Yard Setback min. 6 m, may include the water setback
Lot Coverage max. 100% if > 50% parking is underground, otherwise 60%
Personal Service Shop
Retail Store
Service Repair Establishment
Shopping Centre
Theatre
Veterinary Services
Lot Area N/A
Frontage min. 7.6 m
Front Yard Setback min. 0 m max. 4 m
Flankage Yard Setback min. 0 m 4 m
Side Yard Setback min. 3 m
Buildings are not permitted in the protected view corridors of all streets that extend to the waterfront shown in Figure 17.1
A 6 m (min) wide public boardwalk easement shall be required along all waterfront edges of the DW zone.
The boardwalk shall be built to match the existing standard of a wooden or hard- surfaced unit-paver in coordination with the help of the City and/or CADC.
Buildings shall be set back no less than 2 m from the edge of the public boardwalk.

All groundfloor uses fronting the boardwalk shall include active uses including commercial, cultural, tourism, or food services. These active uses shall be no less than 12 m deep for the full length of the waterfront frontage.
No less than 50% of the waterfront frontage of the building should include a hardscape plaza between the building and the boardwalk if applicable.
Pop-up uses (e.g. mobile carts, seasonal stalls, kiosks, touch tanks), public art installations, and flexible event spaces are permitted and encouraged along the boardwalk edge.
Any groundfloor uses shall include no less than 60% windows and doors along the entire length of the buildings frontage on the waterfront.
All groundfloor uses on the waterfront shall have a minimum Finished Floor Elevation
of 3.67 m CGVD28

Blank walls at the base of buildings are not permitted along the boardwalk, but underground parking may be permitted behind an active groundfloor facade that has no less than 12 m depth and is not visible from the street or boardwalk.
The amenity requirements in Section 3.14 shall be followed.
For multi-unit buildings, each grade-related unit shall include a patio door.
Encroachments into the public street right- of-way for decks, signage, public art, and architectural features above 3 m from grade at the streetline may be considered by Planning Board.
Permitted encroachments into the setbacks are listed in Table 3.2.
Short term rentals are permitted following the requirements of Section 4.10.
One primary building per lot is permitted.
The minimum building height shall be 3 storeys.
The maximum building height shall be:
The height shown in schedule B for unregistered heritage properties.; or
For registered heritage properties, the height shall be the height of the existing heritage building unless negotiated through a Heritage Development Agreement.
The maximum streetwall height shall be 3 storeys.
The minimum streetwall stepback shall be
2.5 m for all buildings that exceed 3 storeys in height; and
3 m for all buildings that are equal to or exceed 8 storeys in height.
The streetwall stepback may be less than the minimum requirement for up to 30% of the width of the building facing the streetline.
A minimum 2 m waterwall stepback is required above the 3rd storey for all buildings that exceed the streetwall height.
Above 8 storeys, the maximum floor plate area shall be 800 m2 and the maximum tower dimensions shall be 35 m x 40 m.

The minimum groundfloor height shall not be 7.6 m Active Street Groundfloor < Min. 50% Residential P1 Parking > 12 m deep active waterfront use At-grade Parking Not permitted less than 4 m in height and shall be no more than 1.5 m above or below the streetline grade. Registered heritage properties shall maintain their groundfloor height.
No less than 50% of Groundfloor uses in the DW zone are reserved for active uses like retail, offices, institutional uses, tourism and lobbies, and are restricted from uses such as:
Parking, and
Grade-related residential dwellings (except residential lobbies) unless placed no less than 10 m behind an active groundfloor street-front use.

A streetwall frontage shall extend the full width of any streetline unless an at-grade plaza is or a mid-block pedestrian connection is provided or if an existing heritage facade is preserved which is less than the full width. 3.67 m CGVD28 Public Waterfront Plaza >50% length of Building fronting boardwalk Waterfront Groundfloor Use Active Use for at least 12 m depth and >60% glazing
Cantilevered or recessed portions within any streetwall that have a depth of 2 m or greater shall not exceed 30% of the width of the streetwall.
Parking garage entries are permitted if they are less than 40% of the frontage.
Parking garage doors shall be set back no less than 3 m from the front lot line.

The maximum front or flanking setback requirement of Section 17.3.3 may be exceeded, if an at-grade open space, such as a plaza, is located between a front or flanking lot line and a primary building provided the setback is not exceeded by more than 30% of the facade.
See Section 6.8-6.9

other_requirements.requirement_text_raw: A 6 m (min) wide public boardwalk easement shall be required along all waterfront edges of the DW zone.
Buildings shall be set back no less than 2 m from the edge of the public boardwalk.

Blank walls at the base of buildings are not permitted along the boardwalk, but underground parking may be permitted behind an active groundfloor facade that has no less than 12 m depth and is not visible from the street or boardwalk.
Encroachments into the public street right- of-way for decks, signage, public art, and architectural features above 3 m from grade at the streetline may be considered by Planning Board.
Grade-related residential dwellings (except residential lobbies) unless placed no less than 10 m behind an active groundfloor street-front use.
Cantilevered or recessed portions within any streetwall that have a depth of 2 m or greater shall not exceed 30% of the width of the streetwall.
Parking garage doors shall be set back no less than 3 m from the front lot line.
